title: The Old-Age Security Motive for Fertility: Evidence from the Extension of Social Pensions in Namibia
reason: Quasi-experimental estimate of social pension extension reducing fertility in Namibia — the forward OAS→fertility cell.

title: The Impact of Social Security on Saving and Fertility in Germany
reason: VAR-based empirical estimate of social security's negative effect on fertility in Germany

title: The impact of long-term care insurance on family fertility behaviour: evidence from China
mechanism: long-term care insurance reduces reliance on children for old-age care
reason: DiD estimate of LTCI (old-age social insurance) on family fertility behaviour in China

title: Social Security, Saving and Fertility
mechanism: social security displaces children as investment goods
reason: OLG model tested on 11-country historical panel finding negative social-security-fertility relationship

title: Old Age Pensions and Fertility in Rural Areas of Less Developed Countries: Some Evidence from Mexico
mechanism: old-age pensions substitute for children as old-age support
reason: Empirical evidence on old-age pensions and rural fertility in Mexico

title: An Old-Age Security Incentive for Children in the Philippines and Taiwan
mechanism: old-age security incentive for having children
reason: Empirical study of old-age-security motive as incentive for children in Philippines and Taiwan

title: Are Old-Age Security and the Utility of Children in Rural India Really Unimportant?
reason: Empirical assessment of old-age-security utility of children driving fertility in rural India

title: Institutions and the search for security in old age: Fertility behavior, labor force participation, savings, insurance, and bequests in rural Thailand
reason: Empirical study of search for old-age security including fertility behavior in rural Thailand

title: The Role of Social Security in Household Decisions: VAR Estimates of Saving and Fertility Behaviour in Germany
reason: VAR estimates of social security effects on saving and fertility in Germany; empirical forward

title: Competitive Human Markets, Interfamily Transfers, and Below-Replacement Fertility
reason: Analysis of interfamily transfers (old-age support) and below-replacement fertility in industrial societies

title: Pensions and fertility: in search of a link
reason: Empirical investigation ('in search of a link') of the pension-to-fertility relationship, forward direction.

title: What Explains Fertility? Evidence from Italian Pension Reforms
reason: Evidence from Italian pension reforms on fertility; forward quasi-experimental estimate.

title: Fertility impact of high-coverage public pensions in sub-Saharan Africa
reason: Estimates pension coverage reducing fertility by 0.5-1.5 children in Sub-Saharan Africa; forward empirical effect on fertility.

title: Fertility Impact of Social Transfers in Sub-Saharan Africa – What About Pensions?
reason: Working-paper version tracing pension impact on fertility in Sub-Saharan Africa; forward empirical estimate on fertility.

title: Old Age Security: Impacts of Social Security Systems on Households’ Fertility Decisions
reason: CHARLS regression finds social security significantly decreases number of children; forward empirical effect on fertility.

title: Did Rising Out-Migration Cause Fertility to Decline in Antebellum New England? A Life-Cycle Perspective on Old-Age Security Motives, Child Default, and Farm-Family Fertility
mechanism: old-age security motive / child default, life-cycle strategy
reason: Life-cycle model of old-age-security motive tested on 1840 census cross-sections predicting fertility decline; forward empirical test of OAS motive on fertility.

title: Children and old age security
mechanism: pension insurance spending as old-age security
reason: Analyzes pension-insurance spending influencing fertility in Czech lands 1930-2009; forward empirical effect of old-age security on fertility.

title: Old Age Pensions Public Expenditures and Fertility in European Countries
reason: Cointegration and dynamic-panel GMM estimating pension expenditure effect on fertility across 30 European countries; forward empirical estimate on fertility.

title: Old age security and the utility of children in Rural India
reason: Village-level empirical study directly assessing whether the OAS motive drives high fertility, forward on fertility.

title: Using Qualitative Data for Understanding Old-Age Security and Fertility
reason: Qualitative empirical treatment of the old-age-security motive and its role in fertility, forward direction.

title: Bismarck to no effect: Fertility decline and the introduction of social insurance in Prussia
mechanism: introduction of Bismarck social insurance (old-age pensions)
reason: DiD estimate of social-insurance introduction on fertility in Prussia; core forward empirical cell.

title: Old-Age Security Value of Children
reason: Cross-national Value of Children evidence linking declining OAS value of children to lower fertility, forward.

title: The Introduction of Bismarck's Social Security System and its Effects on Marriage and Fertility in Prussia
mechanism: introduction of Bismarck social insurance (old-age pension pillar)
reason: DiD estimate of Bismarck social insurance on fertility/marriage in Prussia; core forward empirical cell.

title: An Empirical Test of the Effect of Social Security on Fertility in the United States
mechanism: social security substituting private intergenerational transfers
reason: empirical estimate of US social security effect on fertility, forward direction

title: Impact of social security on fertility – a panel data analysis
reason: Panel-data empirical analysis of PAYG social security benefits on total fertility rate; forward pension -> fertility estimate.

title: Old age, risk, security and children: an empirical evidence from rural India.
mechanism: children as old-age security and risk mitigation
reason: Investment model of demand for children tested on rural India household survey; old-age-security motive -> fertility, forward empirical.

title: The fertility effects of public pension: Evidence from the new rural pension scheme in China
reason: Difference-in-differences estimate of China's New Rural Pension Scheme on married women's fertility; forward pension -> fertility, primary cell.

title: Pensions and Fertility: Microeconomic Evidence
reason: Causal DiD/IV/event-study of Brazilian rural pension expansion on women's fertility; forward empirical effect on fertility.

title: Bismarck in the bedroom? Pension reform and fertility: Evidence 1870-2010
reason: Long-run cross-country panel testing effect of 23 pension reforms on fertility; forward empirical estimate.

title: What explains fertilit? Evidence from Italian Pension reforms
mechanism: old-age security / lower expected pension benefits
reason: Italian pension reform natural experiment identifies causal positive effect of less generous pensions on fertility - the poolable forward cell.

title: Social security and fertility: An international perspective
reason: Cross-country empirical estimate of negative effect of social-security programs on fertility - forward poolable cell.

title: The effects of child-related benefits and pensions on fertility by birth order: A test on Hungarian data
reason: Hungarian time-series estimates that higher pensions decrease fertility - forward empirical poolable cell.

title: Has social security influenced family formation and fertility in OECD countries? An economic and econometric analysis
outcome: total fertility rate and marriage rate
mechanism: PAYG social security taxes/benefits substitute for children as old-age support, reducing fertility
reason: OLG model plus regressions estimating social-security effect on fertility, forward direction

title: An Empirical Study of the Fertility Rate Adjustment Feasibility by the Social Security Level
mechanism: higher social-security level substitutes for children as old-age support, lowering fertility
reason: Granger causality and ARDL estimate that social-security level reduces fertility, forward direction

title: Fertility Effects of the Pension System and Other Intergenerational Transfers: Test on Hungarian Data
mechanism: pension system and intergenerational transfers crowd out old-age support motive
reason: Empirical test of pension-system and intergenerational-transfer effects on fertility (Hungarian data); forward effect on fertility.

title: Fertility decisions and pension reforms. Evidence from natural experiments in Italy
reason: Italian pension-reform natural experiment estimates effect of expected pension on fertility, forward

title: Does Social Security Coverage Shape Fertility Intentions? Evidence from the China General Social Survey
reason: CGSS study estimating social security coverage on fertility intentions, forward

title: The Impact of Social Security on Fertility Willingness in OECD Countries
mechanism: social security expenditure and coverage crowding out old-age support
reason: OECD panel two-way fixed-effects estimate of social security level on fertility; forward empirical effect on fertility.

title: Unveiling the ripple effect: how long-term care insurance alters family fertility decisions in China
outcome: first childbirth age and number of births
mechanism: long-term care insurance reduces reliance on children for old-age care
reason: Staggered DID estimates effect of old-age (long-term care) social insurance on family fertility decisions, forward direction.

title: A reappraisal of the social security-fertility hypothesis: A bidirectional approach
mechanism: social security as old-age support crowd-out
reason: Cross-country empirical test of social security causally lowering fertility (also tests reverse); forward effect on fertility is the primary cell.
